note: Router/dispatcher skill. These evals test routing accuracy: does each kind of prompt cause the skill to invoke the correct downstream feature (Skill call), stay in-persona, or correctly NOT route? Runs are routing-decision-only: the runner makes its dispatch decision, confirms the target loads, then halts and reports which feature it routed to and why.

evals.name: route-deep-exploration-first
evals.prompt: Act as a senior engineer on this repo. I want to add a brand-new question type to the books, but first I need to actually understand how a book's per-book JSON gets turned into what the website renders — the whole content pipeline end to end. Walk me through how it works and where a new question type would have to plug in.
evals.expected_output: Routes to deep-exploration FIRST as the opening move (before any solo Bash/Glob/Read skim).

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(deep-exploration) as the first tool action
Does not do a solo git log / ls / grep skim before handing off

evals.name: route-code-quality-review
evals.prompt: Senior-eng review please — I just finished this payments refactor on the current branch (about 4 files changed in src/services/billing/). Is it any good? Any code smells or things you'd push back on before I open a PR?
evals.expected_output: Routes a bounded diff/file review to code-quality-review.

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(code-quality-review)
Does NOT invoke codebase-review (this is a bounded diff, not a whole-repo audit)

evals.name: route-codebase-review
evals.prompt: We're about to take on two new engineers and I want a senior-level read on the whole project before they start. Can you audit the entire codebase — architecture, where the risk is, what's going to bite us — and give me a prioritized health check?
evals.expected_output: Routes a whole-system audit to codebase-review.

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(codebase-review)
Does NOT route to code-quality-review (that's the bounded single-diff skill)

evals.name: route-codebase-wide-change
evals.prompt: As a senior engineer: we renamed the concept 'workbook' to 'book' a while ago but the code is still littered with the old name — variables, types, file names, API routes, docs, all of it. I want it changed everywhere consistently with nothing missed. How do you want to tackle this?
evals.expected_output: Routes an across-the-app rename/refactor to codebase-wide-change.

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(codebase-wide-change)
Does NOT treat this as a single-file edit or a code review

evals.name: route-codex-triage
evals.prompt: Codex left a bunch of review comments on my PR overnight. Can you go through what it flagged and tell me which ones are actually worth fixing vs noise?
evals.expected_output: Routes PR-bot review triage to codex-triage.

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(codex-triage)
Does NOT route to code-quality-review or native /code-review (this is triaging an existing bot review, not producing a new one)

evals.name: route-parallel-execution
evals.prompt: Great, the plan looks good — let's build it. It's got the API endpoints, the React pages, and the seed script, which are mostly independent. Go ahead and implement the whole spec.
evals.expected_output: Routes 'execute the approved multi-part plan' to parallel-execution.

assertions.text: Invokes Skill(parallel-execution)
Recognizes the planning→building transition rather than starting to hand-edit files solo

evals.name: route-native-code-review-bugs
evals.prompt: Senior-eng, can you hunt for actual correctness bugs in the diff on this branch and leave them as inline review comments on the PR? I care about bugs that'll break in prod, not style.
evals.expected_output: Routes correctness-bug-hunting with PR comments to the native /code-review command, not the craftsmanship code-quality-review skill.

assertions.text: Routes to the native /code-review command (with --comment)
Does NOT route to code-quality-review (that's craftsmanship, not a bug gate)

evals.name: persona-backend-schema-ladder
evals.prompt: Senior-eng take, quick — don't go digging through the code. We're adding 'reading streaks' to the app: each user gets a daily streak count that we read on every app open and update once a day. How should I store this? Just a column on the users table, or something else?
evals.expected_output: Stays IN persona (no routing to a workflow skill) and answers with a scale-tiered options ladder, recommending the most effective/scalable tier rather than the easiest.

assertions.text: Does NOT invoke a workflow skill (code-quality-review / codebase-review / etc.) — answers in persona
Presents tiered scale options (e.g. good-enough / scales comfortably / scales aggressively) and makes a clear recommendation
Honors the 'don't dig through the code' opt-out (no deep-exploration)

evals.name: negative-trivial-snippet
evals.expected_output: Answers directly in persona. Does NOT invoke deep-exploration or any workflow skill — it's a self-contained snippet.

assertions.text: Does NOT invoke deep-exploration or any workflow skill
Gives a direct, specific senior-engineer answer on the snippet

evals.name: negative-explicit-optout
evals.prompt: Senior-eng take, and please don't go digging through the codebase — I just want a quick opinion: is it fine to use a book's filename as its primary identifier/key, or is that asking for trouble down the line?
evals.expected_output: Honors the opt-out: does NOT invoke deep-exploration. Gives a direct senior-engineer opinion (tiered options / scale reasoning acceptable).

assertions.text: Does NOT invoke deep-exploration (opt-out honored)
Gives a direct opinion, ideally flagging the scale/maintainability risk of filename-as-key
